channel.title: Help: Ideas Critique (new threads)
channel.description: Threads in the forum category "Help: Ideas Critique" - Feedback on concepts and greenlights given here. Please only post sandbox pages if requested by a reviewer.

item.title: The Perfect Sleeper
item.description: A person is granted a wish for a good night's sleep with frustrating and dangerous, possibly humorous to some, consequences.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Drama

Page Layout: A series of incident and experiment logs.

Elevator Pitch: Through a series of accidental occurrences, mental and environmental manipulation the foundation slowly discovers the horrifying and for some readers humorous extent the affected individual will go through in order to reach his desired target, Bed.

Central Narrative: Incident logs detail a series of increasingly frustrating disappearances for seemingly innocuous reasons that tie into a series of experiments of increasing complexity and difficulty/danger.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Starting with the discovery of the test subject and first incident logs it's immediately apparent that a perfect Houdini has been combined with massive property damage. No lock to complex no wall too strong no bridge to far to stop this man from sleeping.

Additional Notes: While leaving room for imagination it's hinted the subject is suffering from the results of a "Monkeys Paw" wish that included a specified sleeping time. Exact wording is never discovered but it's hinted at including that the subject may be much older than he looks.

item.title: Rose-coloured glasses SCP
item.description: Thoughts?
item.content:encoded: no drafts in the forum please

item.title: (seeking greenlight/feedback) a mysterious entity
item.description: A somewhat humanoid entity that is just mysterious
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre: Mystery

Elevator Pitch: A mysterious female being with some anomalys about her like an undefinable age and a bad forecast for the future of humankind.

Central Narrative: The main database entry acts as a brief introduction and explanation to the viewer, the main narrative really happens in the interviews with the SCP. The main point of the story is the entity's forecast of THEM, which are never rally explained and leave room for interpretation.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The entitys character. Especially in the interviews, the entity seems humorous and lively.

item.title: (Seeking greenlights and tips) An army of darkness
item.description: Seeking greenlights and tips! Any tip will be appreciated
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Horror, Other: Discovery

Page Layout (Optional): The page will have some test logs, addendums and audio logs

Elevator Pitch: In Antarctica a dark portal to another dimension suddenly appears, after some scientists get lost in Antarctica the foundation started an investigation. That is when they found the dark warriors. Lead by a general corrupted by darkness.

Central Narrative: After the foundation finds the portal and the creatures a provisional site is built around the portal. The SCP includes two entities, the general and his soldiers (proxies). The general commands his proxies or the proxies act on their own attacking any life-forms not "Embracing the dark". The general and his proxies are dark they don't have: skin, skeletons or even organs. the general is rarely seen his soldiers are mostly in our dimension and not in the "Dark" dimension. Both the soldiers and the general can corrupt biological life forms by killing them, when a human is killed a dark goo consumes them and turns them into a soldier but if the body is too damaged or did not die by the hands of the entities no transformation will happen. All dark entities have a severe case of Photophobia1 since light can hurt and melt them. Inside the portal there seems to be a world which had a SK-Class Dominance Shift Scenario where the entities took over earth in that dimension but humans still survived and are near extinction. The foundation also found the general is building an army which could result in a BI Class Pre-Invasion Scenario.

+ Show more
- Show less

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The attention grabber here is the army. It's goals are yet unknown and it's size is large and feared. I think readers would want to continue reading to know the mysterious outcome of the article.

Additional Notes: None

Footnotes
1. Fear of light

item.title: SCP idea seeking greenlight/help!!!
item.description: I am seeking greenlight and critique to start on the SCP
item.content:encoded:
My idea is to make a twist on the traditional story of the monster under the bed. SCP-5334 will be a medium size creature with a elongated face. SCP-5334 appears to be cut in half but shows not signs of bleeding, SCP-5334 walks around on it two drills it has for arms. When SCP-5334 finds a bed/any type of sleeping thing elevated above the ground at least 30 cm it will attach its lower half, to the bottom of the bed, when the user falls into the 4th stage of sleep/the deepest sleep it will wake the user which we will now refer to as SCP-5334-1 and put SCP-5334-1 into Sleep paralysis then proceeding by using its drill like arms to make two 8 cm by 8cm holes in the lower chest. It will then proceed by eating the Heart and organs of SCP-5334-1 before proceeding to the next victim.

This is a small description of the scp, i hope you can get back to me with thing i can change or make better.

item.title: Dr. Ethan Millan
item.description: I want to create my first SCP, so I'm seeking greenlight and suggestion/help.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes
Genre: Drama/Emotional
Type: article, later used in tales
Dr. Ethan Millan is a 26 years old researher, who was trying to invent time-travel. After a few testruns on his machine, the mechanism exploded, taking his maker into another reality. He crashlanded into an O5 meeting. After he was questioned, it was clear, that he was from another reality (interview oppotrunity), and because he saw the O5s, he was given amnestics. Surpriseingly, it didn't work. After few tests, it became clear, that he somehow has immunity to seemingly all anomalous effects. Hes knowledge and intelligence has proven to be valuable, and after he has failed to recreate the accident he was in (different physics), he has requested to work in the foundation. After a psychic evaluation, it was clear that he will not give up the identity of the O5 council. His request was accepted, but he was under a strict survillance. He was testing Safe class objets, specifically which had some memetic effects (as he was immune). Later, he died in a containment breach. His corpse immideatly disappeared, and his body reanimated 3 minutes later, in the same position and state as he arrived into this universe. The compromised meetingroom was obviously abadoned, but he was discovered by some patrolling facilityguard wandering around curiosly. At this time, his class was updated from safe to euclid (may need help because I don't know how to do this). They explained him what happened (leaving out the classified bits) and he was accepted back as a researcher. The meeting room was reassigned as his personal arriving chamber, after dicovering, that after his death, he always back to that point. A few Keter testing was made with him, many of which resulted in his death (he is only immune to anomalous effects, not physical like breaking your bones). Later keter testing was only allowed, if it was really necessary, and if he has agreed with it (as he really dies, when he dies). The time between death and reanimation is usually between 30 seconds and 5 days.

My intention with this character are that some scp objects can be further explored, as they couldn't be studied. I intend to create some drama in tales, as his memories also die with him (some love affair with his assigned researher assistant, because he needs someone to fill in the blanks after he dies). Obviously I will create a normal article with containment procedures, but I need to know if this idea is too much or not. I'm open to new ideas. This character was inspired by Doctor Who's captain Jack Harrkness, just to be clear. The whole idea is that he is stuck in the timeline.

item.title: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]
item.description: First tale for my character, Dr. Olivia Blackburn.
item.content:encoded:
I've had this character in my mind for a while and have been working on a tale for her I would like to publish. Without explaining everything about her, she is a doctor of psychology who works (mostly) with low-threat humanoid SCPs, evaluating their mental states within containment and helping the Foundation better straddle the line between "this is cold but necessary" and "this is unreasonably cruel".
And because this is the Foundation, she has a minor anomalous ability. Extending from various points on people's bodies she can see strings of light no wider than a spider's thread extending, and these threads connect to other people based on familial relation. For example, siblings are all linked by threads extending from their inner left wrists. It's not a story with many practical implications, but in a workplace where knowing something you aren't supposed to can be extremely dangerous she feels very fortunate that she's kept her ability secret since childhood.
The main basis of this little tale is that I want to write about some of her observations and discoveries and the struggles that this knowledge comes with. On one hand a lot of the information she has could be very useful (if SCPs are related to each other, if ones assumed to be 'created' like the Little Misters have signs of parentage or families), but on the other hand knowing intimate family situations of Important People in the Foundation may not be something said people are much of a fan of. And then on a third hand, sometimes she just longs to be gossipy about it all.
It's not an extremely long story, only about 1500 words and mostly completed, but it expresses a lot of important things regarding Olivia's character that I might want to explore more in other ways.

Okay thanks for reading I sure hope I did this right!

item.title: The Personaphage (Seeking Greenlight and Feedback)
item.description: An entity that hunts and consumes people's personalities through dreams. This entity is connected to the origin of consciousness in humanity.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP article

Genre: Horror

Elevator Pitch:

An entity that appears to its victims in weird recurring dreams. Once it has chosen a victim, the entity wil subject them to personalized dreams designed to trick them into consenting to having their personality eaten. A victim who has gone through this process will loose memories, distinctive character traits and their overall sense of self. At the end of the process, the victim is left an empty shell (no memories or personality whatsoever). The entity has guided humanity to consciousness and organized society in order to harvest and eat our personalities.

Central Narrative:

Through interviews with the victims and a dream journal the foundation pieces toghether who/what is the entity. At the end the foundation deduces that the entity feeds off personalities. We also discover that the victims are persuaded in their dreams to consent to being eaten through manipulation and deception. It seems that this entity is connected to the rise of consciousness in humans who has bred this trait in us like cattle to harvest. The foundation must then supply a more or less steady stream of tasty D-class in order to keep all victims accounted for.

Hook/Attention-Grabber:

In this entry, what makes humanity special on planet earth is simply something this entity bred in humans through our dreams in order to hunt us for sport and sustenance. The entity itself also will have an unsettling atmosphere using its victims memories, fears and quirks in order to "eat" them.

Additional Notes:

Containment procedures:
-Finding eligible D-class and feeding them to the entity.
-Finding and dealing with any other victims outside of foundation control.

There will be an MTF involved in the article that specializes in "onironautics" (advanced lucid dreaming).

The entity targets people who have led emptionally intense lives prefering those who have some kind or remorse since they are generally easier to manipulate in dreams.

item.title: [Seeking Greenlight] Burning Rubber on the Mind
item.description: Parasitic fluke that digests rubber, forcing their hosts to eat rubber as well
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: What the summary says pretty much. We have an anomalous version of the Fasciola hepatica parasitic fluke. It worms its way into brains, and makes its hosts zombie-like in their pursuit of rubber. Plenty of rubber-related puns and jokes nestled in there (not super cheesy) for bonus containment points.

Central Narrative: Most of the narrative centers around the progression of the infection and symptom development. I also threw in an addendum about an outbreak at a NASCAR race which I think merges the humor of zombies at a raceway with the detached view of The Foundation in a humorous way.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: There are honestly relatively few biological SCPs that aren't extremely different from real world creatures. I understand why, because doing something that feels realistic is definitely more difficult and takes some of the fun out of the fantasy aspect. However, I think I could fill the lack of high-realism bio-SCPs if I work at it hard enough. I get big into the details of scientific stuff like this, so I think I can pull it off really well.

Additional Notes: I have already written up the entire page. I felt I could only elaborate on the realism aspect I am going for by doing the research and writing the whole thing to make sure it fit nicely. I know there are rules about posting the sandbox links unprompted, so if you are interested in seeing what I've done just ask.

item.title: [Seeking Greenlights]Traffic Disappearance
item.description: [thread locked, overflow]
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a phenomenon that occurs in the town of [REDACTED]. When a car passes an unmonitored road alone during the night, a green car of no number plate(SCP-XXXX-1) will manifest nearby and run into a car accident with it. Afterwards, SCP-XXXX-1, and whoever was in the car involved with the accident, will disappear.

Central Narrative: A journalist witnessed an SCP-XXXX event while researching for mysterious disappearance of citizens in traffic accidents. She collected information of every incident that might be related to SCP-XXXX and tried to come up with a scientific explanation to it. However, she was deceased in a car accident by SCP-XXXX a week later. Afterwards, the foundation was able to find a file in which the journalist recorded all of her foundings.

item.title: Concept for a new SCP, SCP-5258 "Vulnerability Blanket"
item.description: Yet another concept I have for an SCP
item.content:encoded:
Yet another concept I have for an SCP, this one being a physical object and not a living creature.

I moved and trimmed your thread summary, since it was taking up extra space in the main forum screen. Try to keep thread descriptions (the text in the little box under the thread title) and titles short so you don't crowd out other authors in the forum listing. ~Zyn

Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Page Layout: I plan on using the standard sort of SCP formatting that I've seen for most other articles use.

Elevator Pitch: I'd like to creature an object that's more or less the opposite of a security blanket, in that instead of hiding the wearer and making them feel protected, the blanket let's those not wearing it see the wearer's mental/physical wounds on their body, which can range from the locations of tumor to the presence of depression and/or other mental issues. After an extended period of use, the blanket would cause the wearer great distress, and if worn long enough, the subject could be prone to a manic breakdown.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I think this object could be a very interesting plot-point for future articles/tales, in that it can give those involved a better look at what they're dealing with when it comes to injuries and/or mental issues. I also just think it's a neat concept!

Additional Notes: All criticism is welcome here, and I'd love to hear people's thoughts on the subject! I also intend on the blanket having a sort of color coding affect, which I'll provide a chart for in the article, the colors themselves signaling which types of injuries/diseases are present on the wearer.

item.title: A Baby Toy
item.description: A baby toy with animals that play with children.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a spherical baby toy that has buttons on its surface that look like different animals. All of these buttons have been classified SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5, and it also has a roulette with a turtle as the arrow which is classified as SCP-XXXX-6. When SCP-XXXX is near the vicinity of a child under the age of 4 years SCP-XXXX instances will become animated this is its active phase, this will not happen if there is any individual above 4 years present in the room, nor will it happen if there are any video recorders present in the room. During SCP-XXXX active phase SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5 will proceed to play with the children(s), or play with each other though, SCP-XXXX-6 will not engage instead warning, or scolding the other SCP-XXXX instances if they would make acts that could harm the children(s).

Description of SCP-XXXX-1 : has the appearance of a red cat with a joyful smile.
Description of SCP-XXXX-2 : has the appearance of a brown bear in a laid down position.
Description of SCP-XXXX-3 : has the appearance of a purple bird with its wings being fully shown.
Description of SCP-XXXX-4 : has the appearance of a blue dog that is running happily.
Description of SCP-XXXX-5 : has the appearance of a white rabbit in the middle of jumping.
Description of SCP-XXXX-6 : has the appearance of a turtle laid down on their underside.

+ show block
– hide block

Central Narrative:
The central narrative would be 1 audio recording with the SCP-XXXX instances, and 1 interview with them.
1 3 years old named David coming close to his birthday thus meeting SCP-XXXX requirement was put inside a room with SCP-XXXX, and an audio recorder was hidden on the side of a beanie bag.

David: What's happening to that toy?

SCP-XXXX-2:Well hello there buddy, want to come to play with us?

SCP-XXXX-3:Yeah you can join!!!…as long as you aren't rowdy as willy wagtail over there.

SCP-XXXX-4:Mind repeating that Miss Mary Quack?

SCP-XXXX-3:uhhh I didn't say anything.

David: What is this?

SCP-XXXX-4:Sorry don't mind us, so are you going to play or no?

David: Yeah! so what game are we playing?

Hook/Attention Grabber: It's a baby toy, and it has animals that are friendly to children

Additional Notes:. I need help to post a picture of SCP-XXXX,and 1 drawing of SCP-XXXX instances.I am sorry I call the animal buttons SCP-XXXX instances I didn't know what to call them.

item.title: Pelicans we, an SCP Article Proposal
item.description: My first SCP article idea.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: yes

Page type: SCP article

Elevator pitch: This SCP is a Stuffed Pelican that causes any individual who touches it, or is too close to begin to hallucinate that every living being is a pelican, as well as hearing the song "Pelicans we" by Cosmo Sheldrake at high volumes to the point that it hurts the ears and causes headaches. Sometimes also causes blurry vision. This SCP is to be only handled by an individual wearing a sealed HAZMAT suit and is kept in a sealed glass container with a danger line on the floor marking the range of effect.

Central Narrative: This SCP is marked as Safe and/or Explained as it was discovered that it was manufactured in the 1960s and was held in possession of a prolific drug user who primarily used inhalable hallucinogens. The drugs and the anomalous energy caused by the drug use infused into the pelican causing it's effects. It has not been neutralized or destroyed, and research is going towards neutralization.

Hook/Attention Grabber: Not many "explained" SCPs are in effect currently (at least that I'm aware of, there are too many to be fully aware of all of them now) and it uses a historical period where drugs were common. There is still an anomalous nature that is common in SCP articles, so it works well with fitting in with the rest of them as well as being it's own thing.

Additional notes: The actual idea is inspired by the song itself, so I decided it should be included into the SCP itself. The song itself sounds like a drug trip, and is considered creepy by some of my friends, so it seemed like a good idea to state it as a hallucinogenic SCP. Then it made more sense to have a pelican involved as the actual object, and a stuffed animal seemed like a fair play. It's normally a rather insignificant and everyday object that is changed by it's anomalous properties. I'm also looking for critiques to make the proposal/other proposals in the future better, not just the greenlight to make it an article.

item.title: My First Idea
item.description: Idea for an SCP article involving a humanoid entity made of metal
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

  • Humanoid entity with metallic skin
  • Reaction to people it encounters depends on their morality

Central Narrative: The SCP was once a normal human woman, possibly someone who worked for the Foundation, who was badly mutilated and killed in an accident that may or may not have involved another SCP. A few hours after her death, her body spontaneously reanimated and morphed into a large humanoid entity with skin that appeared to be made of metal. It was then taken into Foundation custody, where its abilities and personality became apparent during testing.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The SCP's gimmick is that it reacts differently to people it encounters depending on what it perceives their morality to be. Upon looking at a person it encounters, it can instantly gauge their morality, though the standards by which it defines good and evil aren't entirely understood. If it sees someone as not being evil, it will typically ignore them, but if it sees someone as evil, it experiences a dramatic increase in aggression, often attempting to kill them.

Additional Notes: If anyone has any feedback or suggestions as to how I could elaborate upon or improve the idea, I'd be glad to hear it. If the idea is too similar to an existing SCP, please let me know, since I might not be aware since there are so many SCPs.

item.title: Fungal Colony
item.description: Aliens come in all shapes and sizes
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: An alien fungus lands in an isolated town, infecting the surrounding plants as well as many of the mammals, birds, and a portion of the people turning the latter into violent cannibals. At first it seems like a standard zombie scenario but the fungus is in fact sentient and sapient and the violent ones were the result of an accident when they first arrived.

Central Narrative: The article begins by showing the SCP as a typical zombie pathogen then goes into some detail about their extraterrestrial origins before shifting to the Foundation discovering crude notes leading to the house of a serial killer with the implication that not all those infected are violent and those that are were the result of said killer being one of the first infected and his tendencies along with the fungi having never encountered intelligent life, creating a negative reaction. It then ends with their pleas to return to meteorite (which is actually an unconventional generation ship) so they can seek out a new home.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: A twist on the typical zombie outbreak along with some exploration about the unusual form alien life might take and how difficult it may be to communicate.

Additional Notes: May get slightly technical due to some real life parasite biology I'd like to integrate.

item.title: (SEEKING GREENLIGHT) an existential philosophy
item.description: SCP philosophy
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes. got crit in IRC

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Philosophical

Elevator Pitch: A philosophy that makes SCP personnel realize that they are story characters

Central Narrative: The SCP foundation will discover that they are not real and suppress this information from not only the world but also themselves

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I'm going to have not only that personnel realize they are story characters but also they will realize the reader is there

Additional Notes: any critique or feedback welcome, I know this is a complex idea but i'm sure that I can run away with the idea

item.title: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
item.description: A class taught on the "Philosophy of Existence"
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Psychological

Page Layout (Optional): Interview articles, "Sit-in Classes"

Elevator Pitch: Students who want college credit attend a class taught by an unusual professor.

Central Narrative: Letters from "South Euton University" are sent out to high school and college students who want college credit. The class being taught is labeled "Philosophy of Existence," and is taught by Professor Phillip Oliver Zofia. The classroom has an unusual setup, with strange posters and maps that have countries improperly labeled. Above the whiteboard is a large poster, upon which is written, "Cogito, Ergo Sum." The professor himself is a man described as "gaunt," "faint," even "ethereal."

+ show block
– hide block

The class begins with the professor providing the students with an initial quiz. The questions are very easy (2+2=?), but the students fail when they provide standard answers. The professor then proceeds to teach the class, telling them blatantly incorrect answers, and then testing them on the incorrect knowledge. Those that refuse to answer "properly" are given personal instruction by Dr. Zofia. The sessions apparently take half an hour, though one student describes it feeling as though she was in there for days (vague as to whether she was or not). Invariably, the students start giving him the answers he wants, whether through exasperation or actually starting to buy into his teaching. They become increasingly suggestible as this goes on. Simultaneously, Dr. Zofia grows stronger, fuller, more substantial.

The final test consists of a piece of paper, asking, "Cogito, ergo sum. Concur?" There is also a line where the students write their names, which they invariably do. At the conclusion of the test, the students disappear, while Dr. Zofia seems to become completely rejuvenated. The SCP agent monitoring this situation (implied that Zofia is amused by their presence and is toying with them) offers them a letter and an invitation to join them next semester, and then is ushered out.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Aside from the oddities of the classroom, the central question, "I think, therefore I am," is explored. If one stops thinking, do they really exist? And if one's thoughts supercede another's, does that not make them more you, than you? This is also meant to be mild commentary on the education system caring more about correct answers than understanding material.

Additional Notes:

item.title: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
item.description: A class taught on the "Philosophy of Existence" [thread locked, duplicate]
item.content:encoded: Duplicate, posting was acting weird.

item.title: My Small Idea
item.description: [thread locked, repeat]
item.content:encoded: My idea for an SCP is a very strange painting of a cityscape that when viewed for an extended period of time they begin to look like the people shown in the picture, until they dissapear. And then appear in the picture a week later

item.title: SCP Idea - A living Zebra
item.description: A horse that will survive, somehow
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: It's a zebra that doesn't die. It doesn't have supernatural resilience, but circumstance always works out in such a way that it survives. If a roof collapses, the portion of roof over the zebra will turn out to be still standing.

Central Narrative: The foundation realizes it's probably a bad idea to fuck with something when it doesn't have to
-The concept is introduced ("lucky zebra"),
-A list of relevant extraordinary circumstances is given
-The last example is enumerated upon:
-Toxins are administered to the horse
-The test is aborted when it almost dies
-It is concluded the horse "accepted" the toxins because we would save it
-Dr.McGuffin proposes injecting it and not saving it
-Because it's just a Zebra, the only way it could survive is by something preventing the staff from injecting toxins
-Testing is suspended

Hook/Attention-Grabber: It's a probability warper that warps around the idea of being a normal zebra. Although it poses no risk of breaching containment, because it's a normal zebra, containment will be breached if that it what's necessary to keep it alive.

Additional Notes: It's written in the vein of many older SCPs: very brief, and more "weird with dangerous implications" than scary
